{"step_by_step_thinking": "The digastric muscle is described in several documents as contributing to the depression of the mandible and the elevation of the hyoid bone. Specifically, Document [2] states that 'The digastrics raise the tongue base and hyoid bone and depress the mandible for jaw opening, chewing, and speech.' This indicates that the primary action of the digastric muscle is the depression of the mandible.", "answer_choice": "A"}
